title: Carphobolus axillaris
taxonomy: Carphobolus axillaris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. This family consists of about 135 genera, often referred to as the barberry family. Carphobolus axillaris is a part of the genus Carphobolus, which contains a limited number of species. The exact classification of Carphobolus axillaris may vary depending on the source, but it is generally classified as a shrub or a small tree. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.
uses: Carphobolus axillaris, a plant native to South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
